plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that Module 1 established the innovator mindset and that the next step is translating mindset into personal leadership identity within a complex health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From module completion awareness to readiness for the next leadership phase through a concise recap and forward-looking bridge
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly provides only a closing summary and bridge. This unit should orient learners to what Module 1 accomplished and frame the transition to Module 2 without reteaching unsupported earlier lesson details.
source_refs: Module 1 Complete: The Mindset is Set.
Congratulations on completing Module 1.
the mindset of an innovator
a bridge connecting "Module 1: Mindset"
to "Module 2: Leadership Identity."
plan_units.title: Module 1 Completion and Forward Shift

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that identifying opportunities is insufficient without a leader capable of championing them inside a complex matrixed health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in ideas alone to recognition that execution depends on leadership identity through explicit contrast between opportunity and champion
plan_units.rationale: This is a distinct explanatory beat in the bridge narration: the learner must understand that ideas and opportunities require leadership capacity to become viable.
source_refs: You have filled your Opportunity Radar
an idea is only as good as the leader
champions it
complex matrixed health system
an opportunity is only as viable as the leader championing it
plan_units.title: Opportunity Alone Is Not Enough

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the leadership capabilities named in the source as necessary for innovation execution: political savvy, communication skills, and leadership identity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From abstract interest in innovation to concrete awareness of the leadership capacities required to mobilize change through a high-contrast execution framing
plan_units.rationale: These capabilities are explicitly named in the bridge narration and can be planned as a dense but still gestalt takeaway slide because its value is the simultaneous whole: the 10% idea versus 90% leadership contrast.
source_refs: Having the right idea is only 10%
the other 90%
political savvy
communication skills
leadership identity
get others to follow you
plan_units.title: The Real Work of Change Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: State the explicit transition into Module 2, including its focus on defining leadership identity, communicating vision, and inspiring others to join the cause.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From readiness for change to commitment to leadership formation through a direct preview of Module 2 priorities
plan_units.rationale: This is the destination of the bridge and should land as a resolved forward path. It is directly supported by the source and should not be expanded beyond the listed Module 2 themes.
source_refs: In Module 2, we will move from Mindset to Identity.
define who you are as a leader
how you communicate your vision
inspire others to join your cause

output.additional_instructions: Experience profile: visual-led. Creative rationale: As a closing, on-camera bridge, this moment should be carried by Dr. Naik’s presence and a clear, emblematic bridge visual rather than dense text. It also fits a professional, source-grounded tone and remains feasible given the source gap—static graphics and text overlays without assuming motion assets.
